instruction: Write an essay about how aluminium cans are used in food storage, incorporating detailed explanations of their benefits, such as durability, recyclability, and preservation of food quality, as well as their environmental impact and economic significance. The essay must contain exactly 10 sentences. Include the keywords "sustainability," "corrosion resistance," "lightweight," and "airtight seal" throughout the essay, ensuring that either "sustainability" or "lightweight" appears in the opening sentence but not both. The essay must conclude with the phrase "a cornerstone of modern food preservation." Write exclusively in English, and include at least one English idiom related to innovation. Mimic the writing style of a scientific article, ensuring clarity and precision. The essay must include at least one Chinese idiom related to innovation, written in Chinese.
response: Aluminium cans stand as a testament to engineering ingenuity, offering a lightweight solution that revolutionizes food storage. Their exceptional corrosion resistance ensures that contents remain uncontaminated even under harsh environmental conditions. The airtight seal provided by these containers effectively blocks oxygen, thereby preserving the nutritional quality and flavor of perishable goods. From an economic perspective, the recyclability of aluminium makes it a cost-effective choice for manufacturers seeking to reduce waste. This material's durability allows it to withstand repeated handling and transportation without compromising structural integrity. The industry's commitment to sustainability is evident in the fact that aluminium can be recycled indefinitely without losing its properties. Just as the Chinese idiom 推陈出新 (pushing out the old to bring in the new) describes this cycle of renewal, aluminium production continuously evolves to meet green standards. The lightweight nature of these cans also reduces fuel consumption during logistics, further enhancing their ecological footprint. By combining advanced metallurgy with rigorous quality control, aluminium packaging has become indispensable in the global food supply chain. Ultimately, the widespread adoption of aluminium cans marks a pivotal moment in food technology, securing its place as a cornerstone of modern food preservation.

Conflicts between Format Constraints.new_constraint: The essay must include at least one Chinese idiom related to innovation, written in Chinese.
